operating_ranges.evidence: Maximum element temperature: 1,150°C (2,100°F) for embedded heating modules with Kanthal® alloys
Maximum continuous duty temperature, °C (°F) (in clean air): 1,150 (2,100) for F3 ceramic fiber
Blue line: Fibrothal®, embedded heating shows ~30 kW/m² at 500°C
Blue line: Fibrothal®, embedded heating declining to ~15 kW/m² at 1300°C

degradation_mechanisms.driven_by: temperature exposure at maximum continuous duty
degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Linear shrinkage, % (24 hours at max. continuous duty temperature): 3 for F3
Care is necessary when integrating heating elements due to the potential for corrosion
iron oxide scale can damage Kanthal® heating elements, use a protective cover

fault_conditions.name: electrical_bridging
fault_conditions.description: Formation of electrical bridges from salt or oxide condensates
fault_conditions.evidence: Vapours must not form condensates from salts or oxides, otherwise electrical bridges will be formed

fault_conditions.name: excessive_heat_throughput
fault_conditions.description: Hydrogen atmosphere increases heat throughput 3-4 times normal
fault_conditions.evidence: H₂ increases heat throughput of Fibrothal® 3–4 times

maintenance_actions.name: joint_sealing
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: Prevents radiant losses through module gaps and accommodates shrinkage
maintenance_actions.evidence: place a double-folded layer of ceramic fiber felt between Fibrothal® modules to account for furnace, module tolerances, and module shrinkage

maintenance_actions.name: element_welding
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: Restores electrical connection between terminal and heating element
maintenance_actions.evidence: We recommend using the TIG (Tungsten Inert Gas) welding method if welding is necessary, particularly between the terminal and the heating element

assumptions.statement: Small furnace installations require no special mounting
assumptions.reason: For relatively small furnaces, such as tube furnaces with RAC modules, Fibrothal® half-cylinders or third cylinders, and muffle or chamber furnaces with Fibrothal® panels, no special mounting or fixing measures are typically required

assumptions.statement: Hot resistance is 4% higher than cold resistance
assumptions.reason: Hot resistance is approximately 4% higher, equal to 9.8 Ω from cold resistance of 9.42 Ω
